description_long: This is a vintage, four-engine propeller airplane with a striking red fuselage and wings, accented by white tail surfaces and engine nacelles. The wings are straight and mounted at a mid-level on the fuselage, with large black registration markings 'CCCP N-20' prominently displayed on the upper surfaces. The airplane features a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers in white. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with multiple windows visible. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main gear units under the wings and a tailwheel at the rear. The overall design suggests a classic Soviet-era transport or record-setting aircraft, with a robust and utilitarian appearance.
description: A classic red and white four-engine propeller airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, fixed landing gear, and prominent Soviet markings. The design features an enclosed forward cockpit and a conventional tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad straight wings, four propeller engines, and the forward cockpit with the nose and landing gear visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the red upper wing surface with 'CCCP N-20' markings, white tail, and the profile of the cockpit and engines.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full wingspan, white tail assembly, and the alignment of the engines and landing gear.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, with clear visibility of the engine arrangement, cockpit, and tail structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings, no visible winglets, and no pronounced dihedral or anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines mounted on the leading edge of the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main gear units and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
